name: Alcalá del Júcar and Villavaliente Loop
name_original: Villavaliente y La Recueja — circular desde Alcalá del Júcar por el valle del río Júcar
description: This difficult 18.5-mile touring cycling route from Alcalá del Júcar offers challenging climbs and panoramic views of the Júcar River valley
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Alcalá Del Júcar navigate a landscape defined by the deep Júcar River canyon, characterized by sinuous paths, valleys, and towering limestone cliffs. The region offers a diverse mix of terrain, including paved roads, gravel paths, and unpaved segments. Routes often feature sections along the river combined with ascents into the surrounding hills. This geographical composition provides varied challenges for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Alcalá Del Júcar The most popular touring cycling route is Villa del Ves y Barrio del Santuario — circular de gravel desde Alcalá de Júcar, a 37.0 miles (59.6 km) trail that takes 5 hours 10 minutes to complete. This difficult gravel route features significant elevation gain and offers panoramic views. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Jorquera along the banks of the Júcar River — round trip from Alcalá del Júcar, a difficult 19.1 miles (30.7 km) path. This route follows the Júcar River, providing scenic views of the valley. Local touring cyclists also love the Alcalá del Júcar and Villavaliente Loop, a 18.5 miles (29.7 km) trail leading through the Júcar River valley, often completed in about 1 hour 59 minutes. Touring cycling around Alcalá Del Júcar is defined by the Júcar River canyon, limestone cliffs, and a mix of paved and gravel terrain. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Alcalá Del Júcar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 250 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Alcalá Del Júcar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Alcalá del Júcar?
A: The touring cycling routes in Alcalá del Júcar offer a diverse mix of terrain. You'll encounter paved roads, gravel paths, and unpaved segments. Many routes follow the Júcar River, providing flatter sections, but also feature demanding ascents into the surrounding hills and through the spectacular Júcar River canyon, characterized by deep valleys and towering limestone cliffs.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners?
A: While many routes in the area are rated as moderate to difficult due to significant elevation changes, there are options for beginners. The region offers 1 easy touring cycling route, alongside 15 moderate routes. These typically feature less elevation gain and shorter distances, allowing for a more relaxed experience along the river or through less challenging terrain.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, Alcalá del Júcar offers 27 difficult routes. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063902729/villa-del-ves-y-barrio-del-santuario-circular-de-gravel-desde-alcala-de-jucar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villa del Ves y Barrio del Santuario — circular de gravel desde Alcalá de Júcar&lt;/a&gt;, a 59.6 km gravel ride with nearly 980 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063247896/jorquera-along-the-banks-of-the-jucar-river-round-trip-from-alcala-del-jucar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jorquera along the banks of the Júcar River — round trip from Alcalá del Júcar&lt;/a&gt;, a 30.7 km route with over 645 meters of ascent, offering scenic views along the river.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I see while cycling in Alcalá del Júcar?
A: The routes often provide stunning views of the dramatic Júcar River canyon, with its deep valleys and limestone cliffs. You can also encounter highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5971445" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Morrón – Júcar River Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, the expansive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6300733" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Júcar River Valley&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6162139" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Júcar Canyons&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes may pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5624047" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molinar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5738143" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural pools at the Júcar Dam&lt;/a&gt;, offering picturesque water features.

Q: Are there any historical sites or unique attractions accessible by bike?
A: Yes, Alcalá del Júcar is rich in history and unique architecture. The village itself is a Historic-Artistic Complex, known for its white houses clinging to the hillside and dwellings carved into natural caves. You can cycle past the impressive Alcalá del Júcar Castle, an Almohad fortress offering panoramic views. The area also features the irregular bullring, one of Spain's oldest, and the eclectic Parish Church of San Andrés.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available in Alcalá del Júcar?
A: Yes, several circular touring cycling routes are available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380332344/alcala-del-jucar-and-villavaliente-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá del Júcar and Villavaliente Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a 29.7 km route that takes you through the Júcar River valley. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20130049" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almohad Wall of Jorquera – View of the Júcar loop from Alcalá del Júcar&lt;/a&gt;, a 40.1 km ride offering historical sights and scenic views.

Q: Are the touring cycling routes around Alcalá del Júcar suitable for families?
A: While many routes feature challenging climbs, there are options that families can enjoy. Look for the easier rated routes, which tend to follow the river more closely with less elevation gain. These paths often provide scenic views and a more relaxed pace suitable for various fitness levels within a family. Always check the route's difficulty and elevation profile before setting out.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Alcalá del Júcar?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Alcalá del Júcar is generally during the spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter. This allows for more comfortable riding and better enjoyment of the scenic landscapes.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the cycling routes?
A: Alcalá del Júcar and its surrounding hamlets offer various amenities. The village itself has cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options, including unique cave houses available for rent. Along some routes, you might find smaller establishments in villages like Tolosa. It's advisable to plan your stops, especially on longer routes, as services may be sparse in more remote sections of the canyon.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Alcalá del Júcar?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the dramatic scenery of the Júcar River canyon, the unique experience of cycling through a landscape dotted with cave houses, and the varied terrain that offers both challenging climbs and picturesque riverside paths. The sense of adventure and the historical charm of the region are frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming or other water activities along the routes?
A: Yes, the Júcar River offers opportunities for water activities. You can find tranquil spots for swimming, particularly at 'La Playeta,' a natural river beach. The river is also popular for rafting, kayaking, and paddle surfing. While cycling, you might pass by areas like the hamlet of Tolosa, which provides beautiful river views and access to the water. Always exercise caution and check local conditions before swimming.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in the Alcalá del Júcar area?
A: There are over 40 touring cycling routes available around Alcalá del Júcar. This extensive network provides a wide range of options catering to different skill levels and preferences, from easy riverside rides to challenging routes with significant elevation gain through the canyon.
tips: Great views of Alcalá de Júcar. The viewpoint is located right next to the road, there is a flat area to stop and enjoy the atmosphere.
Beautiful path on the banks of the Júcar river through an impressive valley between Alcalá de Júcar and La Recueja.
Just before Alcalá del Júcar you come to the Ermita de San Lorenzo. This hermitage dates from the 18th century.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The Villavaliente y La Recueja circular route is estimated to take approximately 1 hour and 59 minutes to complete for touring cyclists. However, this can vary based on your fitness level, the number of stops you make, and the specific conditions of the day.

faqs.question: Is the Villavaliente y La Recueja route suitable for beginner touring cyclists?
faqs.answer: No, this route is rated as difficult. It features significant climbs and descents, with sections described as having 'steep ascents' on unpaved roads and forest tracks. It is best suited for experienced touring cyclists prepared for challenging terrain and elevation changes.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route offers varied terrain. You can expect a mix of paved roads, unpaved dirt roads, and forest tracks. Some sections involve ascending on these unpaved surfaces, and riverside paths may also be present. Be prepared for steep gradients.

faqs.question: What scenic highlights will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: This route offers breathtaking landscapes. You'll pass through the dramatic Júcar River Valley, with its impressive rocky formations and lush vegetation. You can also enjoy panoramic views from the Viewpoint of Alcalá de Júcar and pass by the Hermitage of San Lorenzo. The picturesque town of Alcalá del Júcar itself, with its unique architecture and cave dwellings, is a major highlight.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given the challenging nature and potential for varied weather, spring and autumn are generally the best seasons. These offer milder temperatures, which are ideal for tackling the steep ascents and descents without the extreme heat of summer or the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Villavaliente y La Recueja circular route?
faqs.answer: The route starts from Alcalá del Júcar. As it's a popular tourist destination, parking options should be available within or on the outskirts of the town. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival, especially during peak seasons.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the natural environment is generally welcoming, specific rules for dogs on cycling routes can vary. Given the mixed terrain and potential for encountering other users, it's recommended to keep dogs on a leash. Always ensure your dog is capable of handling the route's difficulty and distance.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle this trail?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Villavaliente y La Recueja circular route itself. However, if you visit specific attractions or private areas along the way, they might have their own entry fees.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other official trails. Approximately 11% of the route overlaps with the 'Entrebarrancos' trail, 12% with the 'Ruta de las cuevas', and a small section (1%) with the 'Ruta del Agua'.

faqs.question: What gear should I bring for this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: Given the 'difficult' rating and varied terrain, it's crucial to bring a well-maintained touring bicycle suitable for both paved and unpaved surfaces. Essential gear includes a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit, and a first-aid kit. Due to potential steep sections, consider gearing that assists with climbs.

faqs.question: Are there amenities like cafes or water sources along the route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Alcalá del Júcar, a town with various amenities including cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can stock up on water and food. However, once you leave the town and venture into the valley, amenities become scarce. It's best to carry sufficient water and snacks for the entire duration of your ride.
